resources.string: ℹ️ Game Rules
ℹ️ How to use the app
👥 Players
🎯 Objective
🔢 Scoring
🏁 End of game
📖 Read more on Wikipedia
2 to 8 players.
Look at your own cards without showing them, then announce "Cactus!" when you think you have the lowest total score.
The player who called "Cactus!" scores 1 point if their hand is strictly the lowest (5 or fewer). Otherwise the lowest non-caller scores 1 point. First to 10 points wins.
The game ends when a player reaches 10 points. The player with the most points wins.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cactus_(card_game)
Tap the round number (#) to select who called "Cactus!" for that round.
Then tap each player cell to enter their raw hand score (0–40).
The app calculates the winner of the round automatically and updates totals.
Once a player reaches 10 points, the game is over.
2 to 4 players.
Build the most prestigious city by placing tiles to create districts of 5 colors. Score points by multiplying your stars by the number of districts of each color.
For each color, multiply the number of star symbols by the number of districts of that color in your city. Add 1 point per leftover stone. Highest total wins.
The game ends when all tile stacks are exhausted. Then players tally their scores.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Akropolis_(board_game)
For each color row, tap the top cell to enter the number of stars of that color in your city.
Tap the middle cell of the same color to enter the number of districts. The subtotal (stars × districts) is calculated automatically below.
Tap the Stones row to enter the number of leftover stones (worth 1 point each).
When all cells are filled, the game ends and shows the final results. Highest total wins.
2 players.
Be the first to reach 121 points by scoring combinations in hand, the crib, and during play.
Points are scored during play (pairs, runs, 15s) and from the hand and crib at the end of each round. The dealer holds the crib each round.
The first player to reach 121 points wins immediately, even mid-round.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cribbage
Use the − / + buttons to record points scored during play ("In play") for each player.
The player who goes first enters their end-of-round hand score first — this locks the in-play phase.
Then the dealer enters their hand score, followed by the crib score. The grey cell marks the player who has no crib this round.
The game ends automatically when a player reaches 121 points.
2 players.
Score points by capturing table cards so they sum to exactly 11, clearing the table (escoba) when possible.
Points are awarded for most cards, most golds, 7 of gold, and each escoba. The first to 21 points wins.
The game ends when a player reaches 21 points after a full round.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Escoba
Use − / + to record points scored during play for each player.
At the end of each round, tap a player cell to enter their end-of-round score (0–20). Once the first player enters their score, the in-play phase locks.
When a player reaches 21, the game is over.
2 to 8 players.
Roll 6 dice and set aside scoring combinations to accumulate points. Be the first player to reach 10,000 points.

Common scoring: single 1 = 100 pts, single 5 = 50 pts, three of a kind = face value × 100 (three 1s = 1,000), four of a kind = three of a kind * 2, five of a kind = three of a kind * 3, six of a kind = three of a kind * 4, straight 1–6 = 1,500, three pairs = 750. A "Farkle" (no scoring dice) ends your turn with 0 points for that round. 3 "Farkle" in a row make you lose all the points
The first player to reach or exceed 10,000 points triggers the final round. All other players get one last turn, then the highest total wins.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Farkle
On your turn, tap Add to enter the score of each set of dice you set aside. You can make multiple Add entries per turn.
The last Add entry is re-editable — tap it to correct a mistake before banking.
Tap Bank to lock in your accumulated turn score and pass to the next player. Tap Farkle! if you rolled no scoring dice — your turn score becomes 0.
When a player reaches 10,000 points the game ends and the highest total wins.
3 to 10 players.
Accumulate the most points by selecting card values (0–12) each turn, up to 7 cards. Use bonus cards to multiply or add to your score.

Sum your selected card values. Then apply ×2 if selected. Then add 15 bonus points if you selected exactly 7 cards (Flip 7!). Finally add any +bonus values. Choosing 0 gives you 0 points for the turn. First player rotates each round.
The game ends after a round in which at least one player reaches or exceeds 200 points. The player with the highest total wins.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flip_7
On your turn, tap "0" to score 0 this round and pass, or tap "Flip!" to open the card selection screen.
In the card selection screen, check up to 7 card values (0–12 across two columns). Check any bonus modifiers in the third column (+2, +4, +6, +8, +8, +10, ×2). The score preview updates live.
Tap OK to confirm. Scoring: card sum → ×2 if chosen → +15 if exactly 7 cards selected → + bonuses.
The game ends after a round where someone hits 200 points. Tap a player name in the header to view their column.
3 to 8 players.
Bid exactly the number of tricks you will win each round. Score points by meeting your contract exactly — overbidding or underbidding costs you.
Success: 5 + 2 × contract points. Failure: −2 × number of tricks missed. The last bidder each round cannot choose a contract that would make the total equal the round maximum.
The game ends after all rounds are played. The number of rounds depends on the number of players: (51 ÷ players) × 2. Highest total score wins.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oh_hell
Each round, players bid their contract one by one. Tap your cell in the contract row to choose. The last bidder cannot pick a value that makes the total equal the round limit.
Once all contracts are set, tap your result cell: ✅ if you made your contract exactly, or ❌ repeated for each trick over or under your bid.
Success scores 5 + 2 × contract. Each miss scores −2 per cross. Scores update automatically in the result row.
The game ends when all rounds are complete. The player with the highest total wins.
2 to 5 players.
Cross off numbers on your scoresheet to score points. Each color row runs in a fixed direction (red and yellow ascending 2→12, green and blue descending 12→2). Highest total score wins.
Score per color = triangular sum of checked boxes (1 check = 1 pt, 2 = 3 pt, 3 = 6 pt … 12 = 78 pt). Each penalty costs 5 points. Total = red + yellow + green + blue − penalties.
The game ends immediately when a player marks a 4th penalty, or when 2 color rows are locked (lock = last number checked with at least 5 others in that row).
https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Qwixx
The active player\'s column is highlighted. On their first turn they may tap any available checkbox to cross off a number in a color row.
Non-active players then each get one optional check (tap a checkbox) or pass (tap their name).
The active player then gets a second check. If they skipped their first check, this second one is mandatory (color box OR penalty ✗). Tap their name to pass if they already checked in step 1.
The game ends automatically when 4 penalties are reached by any player or 2 colors are locked. Highest total wins.
2 to 8 players.
Finish each round with the lowest possible total score. Avoid accumulating points — lower is better.
Each card is worth its face value. The player who ends the round gets their score doubled if they are not strictly the lowest scorer. The game ends when someone reaches 100 points.
When a player reaches 100 points the game ends, and the player with the lowest total wins.
https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Skyjo
Tap the round number (#) to select who ended that round.
Then tap each player cell to enter their raw score for the round.
The app applies the doubling penalty automatically and shows the final score per player.
When someone reaches 100 points, the game is over.
3 to 5 players.
The declarer bids a contract and tries to reach the required number of points with the help of oudlers (bouts). Other players cooperate to make the declarer fail.

Score depends on the contract multiplier, the number of bouts, and whether the declarer succeeded. Options like Poignée, Petit au Bout, and Chelem add bonuses or penalties. Scores are zero-sum across all players.
The game ends when a player reaches 1000 points. Highest score wins.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/French_tarot
Tap the next round row to record a new round (3 steps: declarer + contract, optional bonuses, points made).
Tap an existing round number to edit or delete it.
The app computes scores automatically using official French Tarot Federation rules (zero-sum).
When a player reaches 1000, the game is over.
2 to 5 players.
Attract birds to your wildlife preserve by playing bird cards, gathering food, and laying eggs. Score the most points across six categories.
Points come from: Birds played, Bonus cards, End-of-round goals, Eggs on birds, Food on cards, and Tucked cards. Highest total wins.
After 4 rounds, players tally all six scoring categories. The game ends in one session.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wingspan_(board_game)
Tap each cell in a player column to enter their score for each category.
Once all cells are filled, the game ends and shows the final results.
The player with the highest total wins. Green highlights the best score per row, red the lowest.
1 to 8 players.
Roll 5 dice up to 3 times per turn and score combinations in 13 categories. Highest total score wins.
Upper section: sum of matching dice (1s to 6s). Bonus of 35 pts if upper total ≥ 63. Lower section: Three/Four of a Kind, Full House, Straights, Yahtzee (50 pts), and Chance.
The game ends when all players have filled every category (13 turns each).
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yahtzee
The active player is highlighted — tap their column to navigate between players with 4+.
Tap an empty cell to enter a score for the current player. The app then advances to the next player automatically.
The last filled category is re-editable. Tap it to change the value.
When all categories are filled the game ends and shows the final results.
